title: List all high-impact-consequence variants
description: From a 20-row variant-annotation TSV, return the set of variant IDs whose consequence is one of: stop_gained, frameshift_variant, splice_acceptor_variant, splice_donor_variant. Tests set retrieval with Jaccard tolerance (>= 0.8 passes).
task_prompt: You are given a TSV file at annotated_variants.tsv in your working directory. It has columns: variant_id, gene, hgvs_c, consequence, impact. Identify every variant whose consequence column value is exactly one of these four strings: 'stop_gained', 'frameshift_variant', 'splice_acceptor_variant', 'splice_donor_variant'. Do not include splice_region_variant or any other consequence. Write your answer as JSON to eval_answer.json with this exact schema: {"high_impact_variants": ["<variant_id>", ...]}. Use the variant_id strings verbatim from the file. Order does not matter.

inputs.description: 20 annotated variants with VEP-style consequence labels

notes: 7 high-impact variants: 2 frameshift, 3 stop_gained, 1 splice_donor, 1 splice_acceptor. Jaccard 0.8 means missing/extra 1 of 7 (intersection 6, union 8 -> 0.75) is below threshold; we want strict-ish recall but tolerant of one near-miss boundary like splice_region. Actually with 7 expected, 6 correct + 1 missing = 6/7 = 0.857 PASS; 5 correct + 1 missing = 5/8 = 0.625 FAIL.
